title: narcissus pseudonarcissus f. cambricus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus f. cambricus, also known as the Welsh daffodil or Cader daffodil, is a subspecies of the wild daffodil, characterized by its distinctive yellow trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Narcissus pseudonarcissus f. cambricus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the flowers or bulbs to create teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
